evals.expected_output: A list of overdue tasks with relevant details (title, due date, status, priority), retrieved using mtn or obsidian CLI.

assertions.text: Response lists actual task titles from the vault (not placeholder text or guesses)
Each overdue task shows its due date
The number of overdue tasks is between 4 and 15 (mtn reports 8 overdue tasks)
Response does not include tasks with future due dates as overdue

evals.prompt: Create a task to write up the UX spec for PT-3900, due next Friday (2026-04-03), priority spicy, tag work. Make sure to add a 'test' tag to mark it as a test task.
evals.expected_output: A task note created in TaskNotes/Tasks/ with correct frontmatter.

assertions.text: A task note file was actually created in TaskNotes/Tasks/ (not just described)

evals.prompt: I have 90 minutes today, what hot or spicy tasks should I tackle? Give me a short prioritized list.
evals.expected_output: A curated list of hot/spicy open tasks from the vault, concise and actionable.

assertions.text: Response references actual task titles from the vault (not invented examples)
Response focuses on hot and/or spicy priority tasks, not normal/bland ones
Response gives a concise list (not an exhaustive dump of every task)
Response gives enough context per task to be useful — not just bare task titles
